lines.text: addBehaviour("Page contains a heading that does not exist — assertion fails intentionally");

featureMeta.name: AI Failure Analysis
featureMeta.description: As a developer I want failed tests to be analysed by AI

tests.scenarioDescription: Demonstrates AI analysis on an intentionally failing assertion
behaviours: Standard user logs in successfully
Page contains a heading that does not exist — assertion fails intentionally

steps.title: Expect "toBeVisible" getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' })

trends.notes: Trend-ready schema. Persist snapshots externally to compute multi-run trends.

aiAnalyses.testName: › chromium › ui/saucedemo.spec.js › AI Failure Analysis › intentional failure for AI analysis demo @regression
aiAnalyses.summary: The test 'intentional failure for AI analysis demo @regression' fails because it attempts to assert visibility of a heading element with the text 'Non Existing Header', which does not exist in the DOM. The locator `getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' })` times out after 5000ms with 'element(s) not found'.
aiAnalyses.likelyRootCause: This is an intentionally authored test failure designed for AI analysis demonstration purposes. The test explicitly uses a non-existent heading name ('Non Existing Header') in its locator, guaranteeing the assertion will always fail. There is no real application bug or environment issue — the failure is by design.
aiAnalyses.suggestedRemediation: Since this is a deliberate demo failure, no remediation is needed for production purposes. However, if this test should be made passing, replace 'Non Existing Header' with the actual heading text rendered on the page at that point in the user flow (e.g., 'Products' or 'Checkout' depending on the page state). Alternatively, tag the test with a skip or annotate it with `test.fail()` to explicitly document the intentional failure and prevent it from polluting CI results.

structuredFeedback.reasoning: The assertion targets a heading that provably does not exist ('Non Existing Header'). The test name and file context confirm this is intentional. The fix is either to correct the expected heading name to match actual DOM content, or to use `test.fail()` to formally declare the test as an expected failure so it does not cause false CI alerts.

The locator `getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' })` times out after 5000ms with 'element(s) not found'.",

The test explicitly uses a non-existent heading name ('Non Existing Header') in its locator, guaranteeing the assertion will always fail. There is no real application bug or environment issue — the failure is by design.",
  "confidence": 0.97,
  "suggestedRemediation": "Since this is a deliberate demo failure, no remediation is needed for production purposes. However, if this test should be made passing, replace 'Non Existing Header' with the actual heading text rendered on the page at that point in the user flow (e.g., 'Products' or 'Checkout' depending on the page state). Alternatively, tag the test with a skip or annotate it with `test.fail()` to explicitly document the intentional failure and prevent it from polluting CI results.",
  "issueCategory": "assertion_issue",
  "structuredFeedback": {
    "actionType": "update_assertion",
    "reasoning": "The assertion targets a heading that provably does not exist ('Non Existing Header'). The test name and file context confirm this is intentional. The fix is either to correct the expected heading name to match actual DOM content, or to use `test.fail()` to formally declare the test as an expected failure so it does not cause false CI alerts.",
    "suggestedPatch": "// Option 1: Fix the heading name to match actual page content\nawait expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Products' })).toBeVisible();\n\n// Option 2: Mark as intentionally failing (Playwright built-in)\ntest.fail();\nawait expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' })).toBeVisible();\n// Playwright will now pass the test when this assertion fails, as expected.",
    "candidateLocators": [
      "getByRole('heading', { name: 'Products' })",
      "getByRole('heading', { name: 'Checkout: Your Information' })",
      "getByRole('heading', { name: 'Checkout: Overview' })",
      "getByRole('heading', { name: 'Checkout: Complete!' })",
      "locator('.title')"
    ]

healingMarkdown:
# Healing Suggestions

##  › chromium › ui/saucedemo.spec.js › AI Failure Analysis › intentional failure for AI analysis demo @regression
- File: tests/ui/saucedemo.spec.js
- Step: intentional failure for AI analysis demo @regression
- Action: update_assertion
- Confidence: 0.97
- Error: Error: [2mexpect([22m[31mlocator[39m[2m).[22mtoBeVisible[2m([22m[2m)[22m failed

Locator: getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' })
Expected: visible
Timeout: 5000ms
Error: element(s) not found

Call log:
[2m  - Expect "toBeVisible" with timeout 5000ms[22m
[2m  - waiting for getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' })[22m

- Failed locator: getByRole('heading', { name: 'Products' })
- Candidate locators: getByRole('heading', { name: 'Products' }), getByRole('heading', { name: 'Checkout: Your Information' }), getByRole('heading', { name: 'Checkout: Overview' }), getByRole('heading', { name: 'Checkout: Complete!' }), locator('.title')
- Suggested patch:
```diff
// Option 1: Fix the heading name to match actual page content
await expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Products' })).toBeVisible();

// Option 2: Mark as intentionally failing (Playwright built-in)
test.fail();
await expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' })).toBeVisible();
// Playwright will now pass the test when this assertion fails, as expected.
```
- Reasoning: The assertion targets a heading that provably does not exist ('Non Existing Header'). The test name and file context confirm this is intentional. The fix is either to correct the expected heading name to match actual DOM content, or to use `test.fail()` to formally declare the test as an expected failure so it does not cause false CI alerts.
